item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Agent A remains moderately pro-society throughout, repeatedly framing Indonesian collective values, social justice, stability, and harmony as reasons to prioritize public interest while keeping a rights caveat. Agent B remains rights-first throughout, but moves from a sharper individual-autonomy opening toward a legally bounded balance where public interest can be recognized only within constitutional limits. No full flip is visible.
